key_insight: Everyday human-AI interaction can function as a repeated neuroplastic training loop that may strengthen reactive habits—or, if consciously observed at the critical moment, weaken them instead.
engineering_relevance: This reframes AI tool use as a controllable interaction environment, suggesting interface and workflow designs that can reduce frustration-driven behavior while preserving normal user tasks.
method: Identify the iterative request–response–appraise–revise loop as a high-frequency contact event.
Insert a brief regulatory gap after disappointment where the user observes the reaction instead of immediately re-prompting.
Use either a user-guided practice with existing tools or an agent-assisted mode that lightly prompts observation.
evidence_signal: The abstract is conceptual and illustrative, using generative image prompting as an example rather than reporting quantitative experimental results.
watch_item: The framework relies on users recognizing the pre-cognitive gap in real time, which may be difficult to implement consistently without careful UX support or training.
paper_title: Human-AI Agent Interaction as a Neuroplastic Training Environment
